applied_optimizations.name: Triton fused kernel for element-wise ops
applied_optimizations.description: Fused bias addition + clamp(0,1) + scale + clamp(0,1) + divide into a single Triton kernel. Mathematical simplification: clamp(clamp(x, 0, 1) * scale, 0, 1) / scale = clamp(x, 0, 1/scale) for scale > 1

applied_optimizations.name: BLOCK_SIZE optimization
applied_optimizations.description: Changed BLOCK_SIZE from 256 to 1024, reducing kernel time from 0.329ms to 0.179ms. Tested block sizes 64-4096, 1024 was optimal.

attempted_but_failed.name: torch.compile with Triton kernels
attempted_but_failed.reason: Incompatible Triton version - torch.compile produces incorrect results when wrapping custom Triton kernels on ROCm

notes: ConvTranspose2d (MIOpen kernel) is the main bottleneck at 58% of runtime - this is already highly optimized
Triton kernel overhead from hipPointerGetAttribute calls (30 calls, ~11.5ms over 10 iterations) is significant
BLOCK_SIZE=1024 is optimal for this problem size (134M elements)
torch.compile is not compatible with custom Triton kernels on this ROCm setup - produces incorrect results
